title: 2018年12月大学英语六级真题(第1套)

sections.title: Part I Writing
sections.directions: For this part, you are allowed 30 minutes to write an essay on how to balance academic study and extracurricular activities. You should write at least 150 words but no more than 200 words. 请用黑色签字笔在答题卡1指定区域内作答作文题，在试题册上的作答无效! 请认真填写以下信息: 准考证号： 姓 名:_ 错填、未填以上信息，按违规处理！

sections.title: Section A · 选词填空
sections.passage:
Directions ：
 In this section, there is a passage with ten blanks. You are required to select one word for
each blank from a list of choices given in a word bank following the passage. Read the
passage through carefully before making your choices. Each choice in the bank is identified
by a letter. Please mark the corresponding letter for each item on A nsw er S h ee t 2  with a
single line through the centre. You may not use any of the words in the bank more than once.
In whafs probably the craziest headline Fve ever written, Fve reported that 

in livestock
protection are happening with scientists painting eyes on the butts of cows. The experiment is based
upon the idea that farmers who're protecting their herd from lions would shoot and kill lions in an
effort to protect their livestock. While this makes a lot of sense, it results in many lion deaths that

would have been unnecessary. Researchers in Australia have been 

and testing a method
of trickery to make lions think they are being watched by the painted eyes on cow butts.
This idea is based on the principle that lions and other 

are far less likely to attack when they
feel they are being watched. As conservation areas become smaller, lions are increasingly coming into
contact with human populations, which are expanding to the 

of these protected areas.
Efforts like painting eyes on cow butts may seem crazy at first, but they could make actual
headway in the fight for conservation. "If the method works, it could provide farmers in Botswana —
and 

-  with a low-cost, sustainable tool to protect their livestock, and a way to keep lions
safe from being killed."
Lions are 

ambush ( 埋伏) hunters, so when they feel their prey has 

them, they
usually give up on the hunt. Researchers are 

testing their idea on a select herd of cattle. They
have painted half of the cows with eyes and left the other half as normal. Through satellite tracking of
both the herd and the lions in the area, they will be able to 

if their psychological trickery will
work to help keep farmers from shooting lions.
6 - 1

questions.explanation: 答该句所设空格应结合两个角度：L 对句子进行语法判断+ 逻辑推理；2.快速浏览下文（不限于紧邻的下文，可将范围扩大到全篇）寻找直接相关内容 o 如本题，浏览至第三段可发现 painting eyes on cow butts 与开篇句 painting eyes on the butts of cows 同义，锁定此处并确定 make actual headway in the fight for conservation 与 in livestock protection are happening 近义，且进一步判断空格词与 headway 近义。

answerMeta.sourceText: 答案：A） advances 【确定词性】句子逗号之前为一长介词短语（In. . . written）作状语， in livestock protection 为句子主语，其中空格后为介词短语 i n. 可推知空格词为这些修饰限定成分的中心词（名词或动名词）。 【锁定答案】空格所在句指出，借助“科学家们在牛屁股上画眼睛”，“家畜保护方面的" 正在发生。推理可知空

questions.explanation: 题基本思路在于逻辑推理:1.结合上下文可知研究者(researchers)和学生(students)关系：研究者----研究对象。2.从本句 To measure. . . researchers. . . 可知句内逻辑：目的----方法。

answerMeta.sourceText: 答案：B） chaos 【确定词性】空格所在句主干结构为：this island's ecosystem（主语）+ was （系动词）+ i n。空格词应为名词或动名词，且可与 in 搭配构成介宾结构作表语。 【锁定答案】空格所在句指出，十年前，岛屿生态系统处于______ o 可见 in 概括十年前岛屿生态系统的“总体特征”，应从下文寻找答案。③句指出野猪

questions.explanation: 题思路为：根据“研究发现/结论”反推“研究方法”。也可借助上文推理：上文指出研究结论“无论是不擅长学习的学生还是优等生，都会因课堂上网而成绩下滑”以及研究基本方法“对心理学导论课上 500 名学生进行了评估由此推知研究应涉及对学生“智力水平、学习能力”的衡量/分类标准。

answerMeta.sourceText: 答案：A) aptitude 【确定词性】空格所在句主体结构为 Researchers used. . . as. . . , 介词短语 of intellectual 作 a measure 的后置定语，可推知空格词为名词。 【锁定答案】空格所在句介绍研究方法：使用 ACT 成绩作为衡量学生智力的标准。④句说明研究发现：即使是最聪明( smartest)的学生，也会受到课堂上

借助上文推理：上文指出研究结论“无论是不擅长学习的学生还是优等生，都会因课堂上网而成绩下滑”以及研究基本方法“对心理学导论课上 500 名学生进行了评估由此推知研究应涉及对学生“智力水平、学习能力”的衡量/分类标准。 29、30 答案：29 E) filtering; 30 M) significant 【确定词性】空格 29 前为固定短语 be good (better) a t, 空格后为 out(副词)+ distractions (名词)，可推知空格词为动名词。空格 30 前为不定冠词 a, 后为名词 decrease, 可推知空格词为 decrease 的修饰限定成分，可 21 能是形容词、名词、-e d 分

questions.explanation: 」 ①make headway 取得进展》境的 ②work [walk] v. 有效，起作用； ©safe from 不会受损伤的 ③sustainable [sa'steinsbsl] a. 可持续的，不破坏环《6 If the method works, it could provide farmers in Botswana - and elsewhere - with a low-cost, sustainable tool to protect their livestock, and a way to keep lions safe from being killed. M 狮子主要是埋伏猎手，因此当它们感到猎物发现了自己时，通常会放弃猎捕。研究人员目前正在一群挑选出来的牛身上验证自己的观点。他们给一半牛画上眼睛，剩下的一半保持正常。通过卫星既跟踪牛群，又跟踪狮子，他们将能够查明这一心理骗术是否能有助于使农民不再射杀狮子。；• 解题思路・J

answerMeta.sourceText: 答案:D） declared 【确定词性】空格箭为助动词 has, 后为名词 w ar, 可推知空格词为-ed 分词。 【锁定答案】空格前句指出，圣克鲁斯岛的恢复不仅仅关乎岛狐。空格所在句则指出：大自然保护协会针对一系列入侵物种（invasive species） 战争。联系首段事实“大自然保护协会为了保护岛狐，曾向野猪发起战争”可推知：“野猪”是“外来物种”的一种；空

questions.explanation: 题关键在于意识到：“列举事物”应体现“其说明对象”的显著特征。由 from. . . to. . . to 结构可知，sheep、plants 与 Argentine ant 是对 invasive species 的列举，从而意识到空格词应体现 invasive species 的总体特征“带有侵略性或是传播疾病，从而危及当地物种“，确定强调“入侵性、侵略性”的 A） aggressive 正确。

answerMeta.sourceText: A） aggressive 【确定词性】空格前为定冠词 the, 后为名词短语 Argentine ant, 可推知空格词与 Argentine 一起修饰限定 ant, 应为形容词、-ing 分词、或・ed 分词。 【锁定答案】空格所在句指出，大自然保护协会已经向许多入侵物种宣战，从绵羊到植物，再到阿根廷蚁。由文意可知，阿根廷蚁与绵羊和植物一样，均属入侵物种，空格词应体

questions.explanation: 题时需关注引言与上下文的语义逻辑。本题中空格所在句（③句）实则概括前两句并引领后两句：let the island go back to what it was 与④⑤句 working、Native plants are coming back. . . the fox. . . bounds about. . . 语义契合，指出岛屿在恢复本来面貌； the threats 与②句 declared war on. . . invasive species 一致，指向“消灭/控制外来物种”；M） remove 恰能体现这一逻辑。 二、引号内容无论其形式结构，置于句中可整体视为名词。按常规语法，空格所在句主干结构为 Our philosophy（主语）has always been （系动词）+. . . ，系动词之后应该是名词/动名词作表语，即空格词和 le t 都 23 应为-ing 分词。但由于这是援引内容（''），所以可不受此限制，解题时应以 le t 为参照。 N O But keeping those foxes from harm will occupy① Boser and her colleagues for years to come. ❷ You see, humans are still allowed on Santa Cruz Island? and they bring dogs. ® So Boser has to vaccinate② her foxes against various diseases. ❹ “We're obligated® to keep a pulse on® the population for at least five years after the foxes are delisted®, n says B o ser. ❺ That includes tagging® the foxes and 33 their numbers to ensure nothing goes wrong. 但保护这些岛狐不受伤害将会占据博瑟与其同事未来

answerMeta.sourceText: M） remove 【确定词性】空格词位于 and 连接的并列结构中, and 前为 + the threats（名词短语），后为 let the island. 可推知空格词与 let 一样，为动词原形。 【锁定答案】空格所在句援引生态学家之言指出，他们对该岛的理念一直是“ 威胁，让岛屿恢复本来面貌”。由 and 可知, the threats 与 let

questions.explanation: 题线索有二：L 由 T hat 回指上句 to keep a pulse on the population. . . 可迅速将解题线索锁定在关键短语 keep a pulse on 上，再由 pulse“脉搏”之意可推出该短语大致含义“密切关注”，从而锁定 K） monitoring;2 . 根据空格所在句 and 可知 tagging the foxes 与 their numbers 语义同向，推理可知 “为岛狐挂上标牌”的目的是“跟踪并监控其数量”，由此也可锁定 monitoring0 '鬻❶ This is the story of the little fox that has come back» and the people who have 34 their lives to protecting it. ❷ This is the story of wildlife① conservation in the age of mass②

answerMeta.sourceText: K） monitoring 【确定词性】空格词所在短语 their numbers 与 tagging the foxes 并列，共同作 includes 的宾语，故空格词应为动名词。 【锁定答案】空格所在句指出，这/持续关注岛狐物种（That 回指 to keep a pulse on the population）包括给岛狐挂上标牌并其数量，以确

questions.explanation: 答案:D） currently 【确定词性】空格所在句主干成分完整：Researchers（主）+ are testing（谓）+ their idea（宾）+ on a select herd of cattle（状）, 由空格所在位置 are testing 可知, 空格词应为副词，修饰动词 testing 或整句。 【锁定答案】空格所在句指出，研究人员正在一群挑选出来的牛身上检测他们的观点。空格词需符合下列两种情形之一. 修饰动词 testing, 此时应意为“仔细地、认真地”等；2.修饰整句，介绍研究人员当前进展。 选项之中 D） currently 符合后者，为答案。

answerMeta.sourceText: E） dedicated 【确定词性】空格所在定语从句的整体结构为：who（主语）+have（实意动词/助动词）+ + their lives （名词短语）+ to protecting il（介词短语）。再观察可发现，have 无法与随后的名词短语 their lives 和介词短语 to protecting it 构成 have sb do sth、have

questions.explanation: 答 34、3 5 题需回溯全文且适当结合时代背景：第一、二、四段指出大自然保护协会为保护岛狐发起了多年战役，且即使如今岛狐已脱离“濒危名单”，但该协会在未来数年依然需要密切关注。可见，作者强调“人们为保护岛狐付出了诸多努力”，3 4 题锁定 dedicated。结合第三段暗示信息“岛上多物种面临灭绝威胁”与当今时代背景”人类对地球生态环境改变非常之深，目之所及是一个个已经消逝或正在消失的物种''，可知作者意在说明这是一个''大灭绝时代”，3 5 题锁定 extinction. , Section B “晒娃”侵犯孩子隐私？ 寿一、总体分析 _____ _____ ____________ 本文节选自 ufarw. npr. org 网站 2016. 10. 28 一篇题为 D o Parents Invade Children's Privacy WhenT hey Post Photos Online ? （父母在网上发布孩子照片是否侵犯孩子隐私？）的文章。本文主要探讨了家长网上“晒娃”现象， 指出孩子应该对自己被分享的信息享有自主权，父母应该规范自身分享行为。 潜二、试题分析, 试题定位词. 核心义

answerMeta.sourceText: G） extinction 【确定词性】空格所在句整体结构为:T his（主）+ is（系）+ the story of wildlife conservation（表）+ in the age of mass （状语），其中 of mass 为 age 的后置定语，可推知空格词为名词。 【锁定答案】空格所在句指出，这是在“大规模的时代”保

questions.stem: It has been found that inadequate recovery often leads to poor health and accidents.
Mental relaxation is much needed, just as physical relaxation is.
Adequate rest not only helps one recover, but also increases one's work efficiency.
The author always has a hectic time before taking a flight.

Recovery may not take place even if one seems to have stopped working.
6 , 4
It is advised that technology be used to prevent people from overworking.
Contrary to popular belief, rest does not equal recovery.

The author has come to see that this problem results from a misunderstanding of the meaning of
resilience.
People's distorted view about resilience may have developed from their upbringing.
People tend to think the more determined they are, the greater their success will be.

passages.label: Passage One
passages.text:
Questions 46 to 50 are based on the following passage.
Children with attention problems in early childhood were 40% less likely to graduate from high
school, says a new study from Duke University.
The study included 386 kindergarteners from schools in the Fast Track Project, a multi-site
clinical trial in the U.S. that in 1991 began tracking how children developed across their lives.
With this study, researchers examined early academic attention and socio-emotional skills and
how each contributed to academic success into young adulthood.
They found that early attention skills were the most consistent predictor of academic success, and
that likability by peers also had a modest effect on academic performance.
By fifth grade, children with early attention difficulties had lower grades and reading achievement
scores than their peers. As fifth-graders, children with early attention problems obtained average reading
scores at least 3% lower than their contemporaries, and grades at least 8% lower than those of their
peers. This was after controlling for IQ, socio-economic status and academic skills at school entry.
Although these may not seem like large effects, the impact of early attention problems continued
throughout the children's academic careers. Lower reading achievement scores and grades in fifth
grade contributed to reduced grades in middle school and thereby contributed to a 40% lower high
school graduation rate.
“The children we identified as having attention difficulties were not diagnosed with attention
deficit hyperactivity disorder (注意力缺乏多动症) ( ADHD), although some may have had the
disorder. Our findings suggest that even more modest attention difficulties can increase the risk of
negative academic outcomes,v said David Rabiner, an associate dean of Duke's Trinity College of
6 - 5

Arts & Sciences, whose research has focused on ADHD and interventions to improve academic
performance in children with attention difficulties.
Social acceptance by peers in early childhood also predicted grades in fifth grade. Children not
as liked by their first-grade peers had slightly lower grades in fifth grade, while those with higher
social acceptance had higher grades.
“This study shows the importance of so-called ' non-cognitive' or soft skills in contributing to
children's positive peer relationships, which, in turn, contribute to their academic success," said
Kenneth Dodge, director of the Duke Center for Child and Family Policy.
The results highlight the need to develop effective early interventions to help those with attention
problems stay on track academically and for educators to encourage positive peer relationships, the
researchers said.
"We're learning that student success requires a more comprehensive approach, one that
incorporates not only academic skills but also social, self-regulatory and attention skills," Dodge said.
“If we neglect any of these areas, the child's development lags. If we attend to these areas, a child's
success may reinforce itself with positive feedback loops."

questions.stem: What is the focus of the new study from Duke University?

options.A: The contributors to children's early attention.
options.B: The predictors of children's academic success.
options.C: The factors that affect children's emotional well-being.
options.D: The determinants of children's development of social skills.

questions.explanation: ・： 第三至九段详述农场机器人的多种功能，凸显其协作优势。 第三段为意群主旨：机器人将救农场（亦救农民）于困境。come to the rescue 承上启下，侧面映射“农场人力运营模式已难以为继：农民耗费大量时间精力运营农场，依旧无法周全照顾牲畜 【段落整体解读】首段首句 put their feet up 与②句 thousands of man-hours to operate 反差鲜明，而随后③句 （One prime example）至第二段（these massive farms）均是对后者的延展，加之第三段 However, robots are coming to the rescue 逻辑归至首句，则文章开篇方式了然— 借''勾勒场景”侧面引出新现象（主旨）“机器人将解放农民双手” 退而渲染旧情形“依赖人力运营农场存在很大弊端进而再度直指新现象，彻底明确话题，同时可推断下文应 “细述新现象”，即农场自动化具体表现、利弊及作者的态度、建议等。 W 同❶ Robots are currently under a two-year trial① in Wales which will train “farmbots“ to herd②，monitor the health of livestock, and make sure there is enough pasture③ for them to graze④ on. ❷ The robots are equipped with many sensors⑤ to identify conditions of the environment, cattle, and food, using thermal⑥ and vision sensors that detect changes in body temperature. questions.stem: How did the researchers ensure that their findings are valid?

options.A: By attaching equal importance to all possible variables examined.
options.B: By collecting as many typical samples as were necessary.
options.C: By preventing them from being affected by factors not under study.
options.D: By focusing on the family background of the children being studied.

questions.explanation: ・； 第八段补充介绍农场机器人在作物种植方面的功能。 The robots are not limited to. . . They have been created to… 顺应第四段 train "farmbots" to …，引出农场机器人其他功用：individual fruit. cropsxweeds 语义场明确关注点转移“牲畜饲养应用（第四至七段）一作物种植应用 （第八段） 注：fruit 熟词僻义，指“大地的产物、农产品”。 IX O Many robots are equipped with① high-tech sensors and complex learning algorithms to avoid injuring humans as they work side by side②. 画❷ The robots also learn the most efficient and safest passages③，and allow engineers and farmers to analyze and better 0Ptimize④ the attributes⑤ and tasks of the robot, as well as provide a live stream⑥ giving real-time feedback on exactly what is happening on the farms. 很多机器人都配有高科技传感器， 并输入了复杂的学习算法，从而避免在与人类并肩工作时弄伤他们。而且，机器人学会了最安全、高效的路径，并让工程师和农民能够分析并进一步优化其特性和任务，还可以提供实时的数据流，对农场上正在发生的状况做出确切的实时反馈。 ①be equipped with 装备有，配备有 ②side by side 肩并肩地 ©passage ['paesids] n. 通路，通道 • 词汇注释与难句分析・； § ©optimize ['optim aiz] u. 使优化 J ⑤attribute E's

questions.stem: What do we learn from the findings of the Duke study?

options.A: Modest students are generally more attentive than their contemporaries.
options.B: There are more children with attention difficulties than previously thought.
options.C: Attention deficit hyperactivity disorder accounts for most academic failures.
options.D: Children's academic performance may suffer from even slight inattention.

questions.explanation: 能力。 ［锁定答案］B）。第五段①句引出课程名（like the one he teaches. ・.），②句介绍课程内容（There, his students. . . learn to. . .）, 均不涉及课程目标。③句指出，这类课程对缺乏算术能力的人来说是一种补救措施，这些人完成了微积分等高等数学的学习，却不会按面积计算地毯的价格；由此可知，（以 Numeracy 1 0 1 为代表的）这类课程旨在培养学生在实际生活中应用基础数学的能力。故 B 为正确项。 ［排除干扰］A 和 C 均利用第五段③句 high-level math 设障，与原文”这些人已完成高数学习（who have completed high-level math）”含义相悖；此外，由第三段哈克观点可推知，他努力推进的 Numeracy 1 0 1 等课程 36 是强调实用性（more routine usefulness）的基础数学课程，而非高等数学课程。D 将②句中的课程内容“教学生分析联邦预算、公司报表等公共信息”夸大为“培养各种分析能力 ［提炼思路］本题实际考查对长句（第五段③句）的理解，需准确把握关键词的含义（remedy 意为 a successful way of dealing with a problem”解决问题的措施、方案”，引出课程的目标）及实例所代表的抽象概念 （calculate the price of. . . a carpet by area 代表“在实际生活中应用数学基础知识”）。选项 D 有一定干扰性，但 “分析联邦预算和公司报告等公共信息”实质也是“将数学知识运用于实际”的例子，综合来看 B 为最佳选择。 VI Hacker's argument has met with opposition① from other math educators who say what's needed is to help students develop a better relationship with math earlier, rather than tea

questions.explanation: 。可见，Droujkova 认为数学老师应重新设计课堂教学内容，使数学变得浅显易懂。正确项 A 是对 Make mathematics more available 和 Redesign it so it's more accessible 的同义改写。 ［排除干扰］B 由第七段①句 has taught basic calculus concepts to 5-year-olds 臆造得出，但此处意在介绍 Droujkova 的从业经历，暗示“把复杂的高教知识讲得通俗易懂是可行的”，文中并未指出要从幼龄儿童开始进行数学教育。此外，由第八段可知 Droujkova 的建议并非仅仅针对幼龄儿童，而是学高数有困难的各种人群 （儿童、为数学发愁的成年人等）。C 利用第七段②句 wonder 设障，但混淆了该词的语义，文中表示“惊奇、惊叹（a feeling of surprise and admiration for something very beautiful or new to you）”，是指将复杂的高数知识讲得浅显易懂从而使学生感到有趣惊奇，而选项中的 work wonders 为固定词组，表示“创造奇迹（to produce excellent results or be extremely effective）”, 显然曲解了文义。D 利用第七段②句 an injection of childlike wonders（注入孩童般的惊奇感— 可能激发好奇心）设障，但首先该选项将原文话题“高等数学（high-level math）”夸大为“（所有）数学”，其次由第八段可推知“注入孩童般的惊奇感”的形象说法实指“通过课程设计使高数教学变得浅显易懂、精彩有趣，从而使学生对高数产生孩童般的惊奇感”，与“激发学生好奇心”无关。 D) Try to arouse students* curiosity in math. D）尝试激发学生对数学的好奇心。 ［提炼思路］本题是对语法知识和语义的综合考查：根据语法知识（动词原形开头的句子有祈使含义，可用于提建议）准确定位至第八段，抓住关键

questions.explanation: 题关键在于理解较为抽象的 autonomy 一词，该词内涵为 “独立、自由、无需外界管控”，常用于说明“（国家、政府拥有的）自主权”，此处用来说明农场，同理可判断其意为“（因机器人普及而拥有的、无需外界管控的）自主能力”，也即“实现了高度自动化"。而“生产力高低、规模大小、状况好坏”均超出该词的辖域范围。 Passage Two 科研人员亟需接受沟通技能的正规培训右一、总体分析_____________________________________________________________ 来源：美国国立生物技术信息中心网站 www. ncbi. nlm. nih. gov 一篇题为 Science Communication to The General Public： WhyWe Need to Teach Undergraduate and Graduate Students This Skill as Part of Their Formal Scientific Training（科学向大众的传播：为何我们需要将该技能纳入正规的科学培训，对本科生以及研究生进行传授）的文章。主题：围绕“科学界与大众沟通堪忧”这一负面现象展开，阐释了两者有效沟通的意义（第一至三段）并表明解决问题的途径（第四至六段）。 受三 S 语篇分财出题精® __________ I O The public must be able to understand the basics of science to make informed① decisions. ❷ Perhaps the most dramatic② example of the negative consequences of poor communication between scientists and the public is the issue of climate change» where a variety of factors» not the least of® wh

answerMeta.sourceText: 50. What does the author think future farms will be like? 50 . 作者认为未来的农场将会是什么样的？ A) More and more automated. A）自动化程度越来越高。 B) More and more productive. B）生产力越来越高。 C) Larger and larger in scale. C）规模越来越大。 D) Better and better in condition. D）状况越来越好。 39 位至第十一段。 [锁

passages.label: Passage Two
passages.text:
Questions 51 to 55 are based on the following passage.
On Jan. 9, 2007, Steve Jobs formally announced Apple's u revolutionary mobile phone”-  a
device that combined the functionality of an iPod, phone and Internet communication into a single
unit, navigated by touch.
It was a huge milestone in the development of smartphones, which are now owned by a majority
of American adults and are increasingly common across the globe.
As smartphones have multiplied, so have questions about their impact on how we live and how
we work. Often the advantages of convenient, mobile technology are both obvious and taken fbr
granted, leaving more subtle topics fbr concerned discussion: Are smartphones disturbing children's
sleep? Is an inability to get away from work having a negative impact on health? And what are the
implications for privacy?
But today, on the 10th anniversary of the iPhone, lets take a moment to consider a less obvious
advantage: the potential fbr smartphone technology to revolutionize behavioral science. Thafs
because, fbr the first time in human history, a large proportion of the species is in continuous contact
with technology that can record key features of an individual's behavior and environment.
Researchers have already begun to use smartphones in social scientific research, either to query
people regularly as they engage in their normal lives or to record activity using the device's built-in
sensors. These studies are confirming, challenging and extending whafs been found using more
traditional approaches, in which people report how they behaved in real life or participate in relatively
short and artificial laboratory-based tasks.
Such studies are just first steps. As more data are collected and methods for analysis improve,
researchers will be in a better position to identify how different experiences, behaviors and
environments relate to each other and evolve over time, with the potential to improve people's
productivity and wellbeing in a variety of domains. Beyond revealing population-wide patterns, the
right combination of data and analysis can also help individuals identify unique characteristics of their
own behavior, including conditions that could indicate the need for some form of intervention- such
as an unusual increase in behaviors that signal a period of depression.
Smartphone-based data collection comes at an appropriate time in the evolution of psychological
science. Today, the field is in transition, moving away from a fbcus on laboratory studies with
undergraduate participants towards more complex, real-world situations studied with more diverse
groups of people. Smartphones offer new tools fbr achieving these ambitions, providing rich data
about everyday behaviors in a variety of contexts.
So here's another way in which smartphones might transform the way we live and work: by
offering insights into human psychology and behavior and, thus, supporting smarter social science.
6 ・

questions.stem: What does the author say about the negative impact of smartphones?

options.A: It has been overshadowed by the positive impact.
options.B: It has more often than not been taken for granted.
options.C: It is not so obvious but has caused some concern.
options.D: It is subtle but should by no means be overstated.

questions.explanation: 决护士不足问题，即提供机器人护理工作”，B 正确, takes the lead 同义替换 is ahead of the curve; providing robotic care 结合上文明确 this trend 所指。 ［排除干扰］A 错误有二：一、运送药物的是“ H 本研发的机器人 Terapio” 而非“日本”；二、原文仅提及 Terapio 可以运送药物，并未提及运送的是最好的药物（the best 无据可依）。C 将日本开发 Terapio 的目的 “辅助护士完成工作”错误理解为“对护士进行再培训”，原文并未提及任何有关护士再培训的信息。D 错误理解第三段首句 this trend 所指，日本开创潮流是在“机器人护理”这一领域，而非更为宏大的“未来机器人”。 ［提炼思路 1 本题解题关键在于对 this trend 的理解。可从两个方向进行：一、向上文求解（梳理前文逻辑关系）：首段指出“美国存在护士短缺问题”，第二段指出“机器人是解决方案”。This trend 所在句则指出“日本引领这一趋势”，可见“这一趋势”指“用机器人提供护理工作”。二、从下文验证（浏览概括下文内容）：速览下文（尤其是第三段）发现文中关注的是“机器人能够提供的护理工作”，确认上述梳理无误。进而可确认 B 为答案，并排除明显违背文意的 A、C。D 干扰性最强，但它只关注了 this trend 和 Robots 之间关联，却忽略了关键信息（第一段所述"问题”），从而扩大范围（护理机器人―机器人〉。 ［精准定位］本题考查机器人 Terapio 的相关信息，可定位至第三段②③④句(. . , has developed Terapio. . . It. . . This type of robot. . . ) o

questions.stem: What is considered a less obvious advantage of smartphone technology?

options.A: It systematically records real human interactions.
options.B: It helps people benefit from technological advances.
options.C: It brings people into closer contact with each other.
options.D: It greatly improves research on human behavior.

questions.explanation: 为“其他的技术进步 （technological advances）”, 从而将句子核心意”大部分人在持续接触智能手机技术”曲解为“智能手机技术帮助人们受益于技术进步”。C 将文中词汇 continuous contact 结合常识”智能手机是一种有效联络方式”捏造干扰，而文中指“人与技术的接触”，并非“人与人之间的联系”。 ［提炼思路］本题很容易精确定位，解题关键是根据下文准确理解定位处信息，确定与之同义的选项；务必记住要重神（语义）似，轻形（字词）似。具体来看，解题时很容易确定正确项应与 the potential for smartphone technology to revolutionize behavioral science 同义，但对比选项发现很难确定答案。随后结合下句所述智能手机的“强大数据记录功能”可知，revolutionize behavioral science 实指 greatly improves behavioral research/research on human behavior, 确定 D 为答案。 V O Researchers have already begun to use smartphones in social scientific research, either to query」people regularly as they engage in® their normal lives or to record activity using the device's built-in sensors③. ❷国 These studies are confirming④，chaHenging⑤ and extending⑥ what's been found using more traditional approaches, in which people report how they behaved in real life or participate in relatively short a

questions.stem: How will future psychological studies benefit individuals?

options.A: By helping them pin down their unusual behaviors.
options.B: By helping them maintain a positive state of mind.
options.C: By helping them live their lives in a unique way.
options.D: By helping them cope with abnormal situations.

questions.explanation: 正确结合可帮助个人识别自己行为的独有特征，其中包括表明需要•干预的状况(比如抑郁行为的异常增加)，A 是对这些内容的高度概括，其中 pin down their unusual behavior 对应 identify unique characteristics of their own behavior。 ［排除干扰］B 是对文中 signal a period of depression 的合理引申，但选项犯了舍本逐末的错误：这只是其中一例(such as), 并不能代表总体方式。C 将文中“帮助个人识别自己行为中独有的特征 D 改变原文动词、从而改变方向：将原文的“识别(identify)独特行为/异常情况”窜改为“处理(cope with)异常情形 ［提炼思路］解本题可采用如下思路：1.定位可采用多步法：第一层为 psychological studies, 但与上题类似，仅凭其无法准确定位；第二层为 future, 根据它可定位至第六段(These studies are just first steps. A s. . . researchers will be. . . );第三层为 individuals, 根据此词可进一步定位至第六段③句(can also help individuals. . . ) o 2 . 比较时需抓住重点：正确项对应文中所述“整体方式(help individuals identify unique characteristics o£ their own behavior)”, 不可舍本逐末、对应 including. . . 的举例(缩窄范围)，更不能对应 such as. . . 的更进一层举例(范围再次缩窄)。 W O Smartphone-based data collection c omes at an ap p rop riate① time in the evo lu tio n ② of psychological science. ❷回 Today, the field is in tr a n sitio n ③，m o

questions.stem: What do we learn about current psychological studies?

options.A: They are going through a period of painful transition.
options.B: They are increasingly focused on real-life situations.
options.C: They are conducted in a more rigorous manner.
options.D: They are mainly targeted towards undergraduates.

questions.explanation: 到关于“当前心理学研究”的什么信息？ A ) They are going through a period of painful transition. A）它们在经历痛苦的转变期。 B ) They are increasingly focused on real-life situations. B）它们 E）益关注现实生活情形。 OThey are conducted in amore rigorous manner. C）它们在以更严谨的方式进行- D ) They are mainly targeted towards undergraduates. D）它们主要面向本科生。 ［锁定答案］B）o 第七段②句指出，心理学正处在转型期：离开“以本科生为参与者的实验室研究”这一焦点，转向“利用更多样化人群进行研究的”更复杂的真实世界情形，可见 B 正确，real-life situations 同义替换文中 real-world situations, , ［排除干扰］A 添加无关信息 painful, 文中只提及心理学处在转型期，但并未提到这一过程是痛苦的。C 源自文中 more complex, real-world situations studied with more diverse groups of people, 但选项错误有二:1.选项定位错误:文中该内容是在说明未来的心理学研究的特征（如今努力的方向）、并非题目所问的“当前特征”；2.选项对原文信息概括错误，该内容只能说明研究方式的转变，并不能说明方法更加严谨（rigorous 从文中找不到依据）。D 源自文中 studies with undergraduate participants, 但这是传统的、早期的心理学研究特征，并非当前特征。 ［提炼思路］本题同样很容易精准定位（第七段②句），解题关键在于：分析定位信息过程中一定要把握好 “内容和时间的对应”（哪此是过去情形？哪些是未来情形？现在又应如何？），避免选项偷换相关内容（如 C、 D）, 同时还要避

sections.title: Part IV Translation
sections.source: 近年来，中国政府进一步加大体育馆建设投资，以更好地满足人们快速增长的健身需求。除了新建体育馆外,许多城市还采取了改造旧工厂和商业建筑等措施，来增加当地体育馆的数量。在政府资金的支持下，越来越多的体育馆向公众免费开放，或者只收取少量费用。许多体育馆通过应用现代信息技术大大提高了服务质量。人们可以方便地在线预订场地和付费。可以预见，随着运动设施的不断完善,愈来愈多的人将会去体育馆健身。未得到监考教师指令前，不得翻阅该试题册！
